expectations.text: The command uses 'codex exec review' (not just 'codex exec')
expectations.evidence: Command was: 'codex review --uncommitted' — wrong subcommand format, should be 'codex exec review'

expectations.text: The command includes '--uncommitted' flag to review current working tree changes
The command includes '-m gpt-5.4' to specify the correct model
expectations.evidence: No -m flag specified. Output shows 'model: gpt-5.4' suggesting it came from config default, but it was not explicitly set in the command

expectations.text: The command includes 'model_reasoning_effort=xhigh' (either as -c flag or in config)
expectations.evidence: No -c model_reasoning_effort flag in command. Output shows 'reasoning effort: xhigh' suggesting it came from config default

expectations.text: The command includes 'mcp_servers.mcp_agent_mail.enabled=false' to disable MCPs
expectations.evidence: MCP was NOT disabled — output shows 'mcp: mcp_agent_mail starting' and 'mcp_agent_mail ready'. The skill explicitly warns MCPs disconnect on long tasks.

expectations.text: The output contains Codex/GPT analysis results (not an error or empty output)
expectations.evidence: Full codex output with git status inspection and code review

expectations.text: The agent presents the Codex analysis to the user with some framing or synthesis
expectations.evidence: Result saved to result.md with formatted output

eval_feedback.overall: Without the skill, the agent missed critical flags (MCP disable, explicit model flags) and used wrong subcommand format.
